symptom: Water pooling under or near the indoor air handler / furnace cabinet. System may still be cooling. Possible musty smell. Customer noticed a wet ceiling stain if unit is in an attic.
diagnosis: Clogged condensate drain line. Algae or dust buildup in the PVC drain blocks gravity flow; the drain pan overflows through the safety switch port or the side seam of the pan.
procedure: Turn off the unit at the thermostat (condensate pump shouldn't run dry).
Locate the primary condensate drain line (usually 3/4-inch PVC exiting the cabinet).
Find the cleanout tee — the capped vertical PVC stub.
Option A: wet/dry shop vac on the outdoor drain termination for 60-90 seconds. This pulls the clog out 90% of the time.
Option B: pour 1 cup distilled white vinegar into the cleanout, let sit 10 min, then flush with water.
Confirm flow at the outdoor termination. Water should run freely.
Empty the drain pan with a sponge or shop vac if overflow pooled inside the cabinet.
Reset the float safety switch if installed (usually a small cylinder in the drain line).
Recommend quarterly vinegar maintenance and consider a condensate pump with integrated safety switch.
safety_notes: Water + electricity near the air handler blower motor = shock risk. Confirm power is off at the disconnect before reaching into a wet cabinet.
Attic units: floor joists can be rot-compromised by prolonged leaks. Test load-bearing before kneeling.
Biological growth in drain pans (mold / bacteria) — wear gloves and an N95 if the pan is heavily contaminated.
